facilities.name: Columbia Asia Extended Care Hospital
facilities.editorial:
Columbia Asia Extended Care Hospital in Section 17, Shah Alam is the oldest facility in the Columbia Asia private hospital group, opened in 1998 and reportedly the group's first Malaysian property. Unlike the group's acute hospitals, this facility is purpose-built for extended stays: long-term nursing care, post-hospitalisation step-down, and inpatient rehabilitation rather than emergency medicine or acute surgery. Being hospital-licensed rather than registered under the Akta Pusat Jagaan as a nursing home, it operates with a different regulatory framework and a higher clinical infrastructure than the typical private nursing home.

The extended care model here suits families whose relatives have complex post-acute needs — stroke patients requiring weeks of inpatient rehabilitation, those recovering from major surgery, or elderly individuals with multiple chronic conditions who need hospital-grade nursing rather than assisted-living supervision. The step-down positioning means discharge planning from an acute hospital such as Subang Jaya Medical Centre or Hospital Shah Alam can include this facility as a transitional option before the resident either returns home or transitions to a long-term nursing home. The Section 17, Shah Alam location is accessible from the Klang Valley highway network.

With 310 Google reviews at a 4.9-star average — by far the largest reviewed eldercare facility in this guide — Columbia Asia Extended Care represents an unusual degree of verified public satisfaction for a Malaysian extended care setting. Families should clarify on enquiry whether the facility can accommodate their relative's specific care needs, what the fee structure includes, and whether government panel or insurance arrangements apply. Availability is likely to be more constrained than at smaller private nursing homes, so enquiring early in the discharge planning process is advisable.

source_data.notes: Hospital-licensed (not Akta Pusat Jagaan); purpose-built extended care; Columbia Asia group first MY property, opened 1998

facilities.name: AustinLoyal Care Centre
facilities.editorial:
AustinLoyal Care Centre operates from a corner lot at 1 Jalan Mutiara Emas 3/4 in Taman Mount Austin, a busy residential and commercial area of Johor Bahru within a few minutes' drive of hospitals, clinics, and shops. The home is run by AUSTINLOYAL SDN BHD (registration 1142053-H) and soft-launched in March 2018. Taman Mount Austin is one of JB's most established residential townships and provides reasonable access to Johor Bahru's private and government hospital corridor for acute escalations.

The facility operates in the Mount Austin corridor, where supply of private nursing homes has expanded in recent years to meet demand from the surrounding residential population and from Singapore-side families placing relatives across the Causeway. The registered company number and the 2018 launch date provide a baseline for verifying licence status with JKM. Families should also confirm the current care scope — the types of residents the facility accepts and the clinical conditions it can manage — directly on enquiry.

AustinLoyal carries a 1.9-star rating from 260 Google reviews at the time of writing. At this volume, the rating reflects a sustained pattern of feedback rather than a small number of isolated reviews, and families considering this facility should apply especially thorough due diligence before placing a relative: request independent references from other families with relatives currently placed there, visit at different times of day rather than a single pre-arranged tour, and ask the operator directly about the care model, staffing ratios, and what specific changes have been made to address the concerns that shaped the review record. Request sight of the current JKM licence and any inspection records during your visit.

source_data.address: 1 Jalan Mutiara Emas 3/4, Taman Mount Austin, Johor Bahru
source_data.notes: AUSTINLOYAL SDN BHD (1142053-H); soft-launched March 2018; very low rating (1.9/260) — handled neutrally per editorial rules

facilities.name: ECON Medicare Centre & Nursing Home (Taman Perling)
facilities.editorial:
ECON Medicare Centre & Nursing Home – Taman Perling Branch stands apart from other private nursing homes in Johor Bahru by virtue of its corporate pedigree and scale. Opened in April 2013, this purpose-built 199-bed, four-storey facility covers approximately 57,000 square feet and is operated by ECON Healthcare Group — the largest private nursing home operator in Singapore and Malaysia by revenue, with 36 years of eldercare experience across the two countries. Located approximately 45 minutes from the Second Link Causeway, it is the primary cross-border option in JB for Singapore families seeking an ECON-network placement at Malaysian pricing.

ECON's scale enables standardised care protocols, group procurement for medical consumables, and the possibility of internal transfers to other ECON facilities if a resident's care needs change. At 199 beds, the Perling branch operates at a size where specialist staff — dedicated physiotherapists, dietitians, and registered nurses — can be employed full-time rather than on an ad-hoc basis, which smaller homes often cannot sustain. Published fees start from approximately RM 1,950 per month — competitive for a purpose-built, purpose-operated facility of this size in JB.

The facility holds a 4.5-star rating from 152 Google reviews — a volume that carries real evidential weight and is one of the most substantiated nursing home ratings in Johor Bahru. For families managing a cross-border placement, ECON's group structure means a recognisable name, a published enquiry process, and accessible care plan documentation. Families should confirm which room types are available at the base fee, verify the current staffing roster, and ask what the transfer pathway looks like should a higher level of care become necessary.

source_data.notes: ECON Healthcare Group; 199 beds; 57,000 sqft; 4-storey purpose-built; opened April 2013; ~45 min from Second Link

facilities.name: Lecadia Primacare Centre Sdn. Bhd.
facilities.editorial:
Lecadia Primacare Centre Sdn. Bhd. has operated from 417-425 Jalan Pudu since 2004, making it one of the longer-established mid-tier nursing homes in central Kuala Lumpur. Registered with the Ministry of Health, it operates as a long-term geriatric and chronic-illness facility rather than a short-stay rehabilitation centre. The shophouse row configuration gives the centre more floor area than a single converted house but a more corridor-oriented layout than a purpose-built nursing home — a common structure for KL nursing homes established before the post-2010 wave of purpose-built construction.

Pudu's central KL position means good proximity to Hospital Kuala Lumpur, University of Malaya Medical Centre, and multiple private hospitals — a practical consideration for residents requiring frequent specialist appointments or acute escalation. The address is also logistically convenient for adult children visiting from across the Klang Valley. Lecadia's 20-plus years at the same location provides a meaningful track record for a market where newer entrants are common.

Lecadia carries a 3.8-star rating from 131 Google reviews at the time of writing. At this volume, families should review the pattern of feedback to understand recurring themes before deciding to visit. On a visit, confirm the care scope relevant to your relative's specific condition — geriatric long-term care and post-acute recovery have different staffing profiles — and ask about current room availability, pricing, the staffing roster, and the facility's escalation pathway to nearby hospitals for acute medical events. The 20-year operating history and MOH registration provide a baseline of institutional continuity worth verifying against the current licence.

source_data.notes: Since 2004; shophouse row; MOH-registered; geriatric/chronic illness LTC focus

facilities.name: Synergy Care Centre
facilities.editorial:
Synergy Care Centre is a 24-hour nursing home at 15 Jalan USJ Sentral 3 in the USJ Sentral commercial precinct of Subang Jaya, operated by Nursing Synergy Sdn Bhd. The centre is led by Dr. Pathma, who is referenced in multiple Google reviews as being personally involved in resident care and directly responsive to family enquiries — a level of named physician accessibility that is notable in a market where medical oversight is often described generically without specifying who provides it or how frequently. The USJ Sentral address is a commercial-zone setting with retail and food access nearby.

The centre provides confirmed 24-hour registered nurse coverage, a standard that smaller nursing homes sometimes list without maintaining in practice. Nursing Synergy Sdn Bhd is listed with a second contact line and a second location, suggesting some degree of multi-site operation; families should confirm the specific arrangements for their relative's branch directly on enquiry. The USJ location provides access to Subang Jaya Medical Centre and Sunway Medical Centre for acute escalations, and is accessible for families in USJ, Subang, and Shah Alam.

Synergy Care holds a 4.8-star rating from 127 Google reviews at the time of writing — a strong signal reflecting consistent family satisfaction at a volume that provides meaningful evidential confidence for a private nursing home in this price tier. Families visiting should confirm room type availability, the current pricing structure and what is included in the monthly fee, the specific scope of Dr. Pathma's clinical involvement, and whether physiotherapy and specialist consultations are available on-site or by referral. An unannounced follow-up visit after an initial scheduled tour is advisable before committing to placement.

source_data.address: 15 Jalan USJ Sentral 3, USJ Sentral, Subang Jaya
source_data.notes: Nursing Synergy Sdn Bhd; Dr. Pathma named as lead physician in reviews; 24hr RN confirmed; second location exists

facilities.name: My Precious Home Care Elder Care
facilities.editorial:
My Precious Home Care Elder Care is a residential nursing home at 27 Jalan USJ 1/3B, Grandville, Subang Jaya. The facility was founded by a doctor who continues to conduct regular visits and health monitoring for residents — a clinically-grounded founding model that distinguishes it from nursing homes operated primarily by administrators. The group runs at least two branches, with a second site in Kampung Tunku, Petaling Jaya; group information is published at myprecioushomecare.com.

The care scope at the USJ branch covers long-term and short-term stays, 24-hour personal care, dementia care, palliative care, and in-house physiotherapy for rehabilitation — a range that places it toward the higher end of the care spectrum among Subang-area nursing homes. The Grandville location in USJ 1 is a residential-zone setting typical of converted terrace or semi-detached houses in this part of Subang. Proximity to Subang Jaya Medical Centre and Sunway Medical Centre provides reasonable escalation options for acute events.

The facility holds a 4.7-star rating from 122 Google reviews — a strong signal at a volume that reflects sustained family satisfaction rather than a short early-review period. With a named founder-physician, a published website, and a two-branch structure, My Precious Home Care is one of the more transparently presented small-group operators in the USJ and Subang Jaya catchment. Families should confirm which services are included in the monthly fee versus billed additionally, the current availability of the in-house physiotherapist, and visit both the USJ and Kampung Tunku branches if the PJ location is geographically preferable before committing to a placement.

source_data.notes: Founded by doctor; 2 branches (USJ + Kampung Tunku PJ); website myprecioushomecare.com; care scope includes dementia, palliative, in-house physio

facilities.name: PJ Senior Care
facilities.editorial:
PJ Senior Care is a nursing home at 23 Jalan 14/56 in Seksyen 14, Petaling Jaya — a short street away from the Amazing Grace Retirement Home on Jalan 14/55, making Section 14 a recognisable cluster of eldercare options within central PJ. The facility's stated focus is geriatric, orthopaedic, and neurological rehabilitation, with a team the operator's website describes as comprising physiotherapists, nurses, and doctors trained specifically in stroke and mobility care. The rehabilitation-centred approach positions PJ Senior Care differently from custodial nursing homes where physiotherapy is an optional add-on.

Section 14 gives PJ Senior Care good access to Assunta Hospital and the PJ Old Town medical cluster, which is relevant for residents requiring regular specialist review or who need acute escalation. The physio-centred model means the facility may be particularly suited to stroke survivors and post-orthopaedic-surgery residents whose primary care objective is structured rehabilitation — though families should confirm whether long-term nursing care is also available here, as the two have different admission criteria and pricing structures.

PJ Senior Care holds a 5.0-star rating from 105 Google reviews at the time of writing — a strong signal at a volume that provides meaningful confidence. Families considering a placement should clarify whether the facility accommodates long-term nursing care alongside rehabilitation, confirm the current physiotherapy schedule and which therapists are on staff, and enquire about pricing — which is not published online and is quoted on request. A visit to observe rehabilitation sessions in progress and meet the care team is the most informative step before committing to placement.

source_data.notes: Rehab-focused (geriatric, orthopaedic, neurological); physio+nurse+doctor team; near Amazing Grace Retirement Home on Jalan 14/55

facilities.name: Amazing Grace Caring Home
facilities.editorial:
Amazing Grace Caring Home is a multi-branch nursing home network that has been operating since 2007, guided by Christian values and accepting residents of all backgrounds and faiths. Branches span Kuala Lumpur and Selangor, covering Taman Tan Yew Lai, Taman Yarl, Section 14 Petaling Jaya, and SS19 Subang Jaya — a spread that makes the group one of the more geographically distributed smaller nursing home networks in the Klang Valley. The Taman Tan Yew Lai branch, near the Old Klang Road corridor, is the primary listed location. This network operates independently and is separate from the Amazing Grace (Subang) operator.

The group publishes an indicative price band of RM 2,001 to RM 4,000 per month depending on room type — shared twin, triple, or single with attached bathroom — which positions it in the mid-to-upper tier of the Klang Valley market. Published price transparency of this kind allows families to assess affordability before making a site visit, which is relatively uncommon among smaller nursing home operators. The multi-branch structure means families may find a branch location closer to home than the Taman Tan Yew Lai listing.

Amazing Grace Caring Home holds a 4.9-star rating from 92 Google reviews at the time of writing — a strong signal at a volume that reflects sustained family satisfaction since 2007. Families should contact the group to confirm which branch currently has availability matching their relative's care needs, visit the specific branch rather than relying on the listing location, and ask about the care staff mix, clinical escalation pathway for medical events, and what is included within the published fee range. For families with a preference for a faith-aligned care environment, the group's Christian ethos may be a meaningful factor alongside geography and pricing.

source_data.address: Taman Tan Yew Lai, Selangor (multi-branch: also Taman Yarl KL, Section 14 PJ, SS19 Subang Jaya)
source_data.notes: Since 2007; Christian values; multi-branch KL/PJ/Subang; DIFFERENT network from Amazing Grace (Subang) operator already processed in batch 1

facilities.name: Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Muhammad Ibraahim (PJMI)
facilities.editorial:
Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Muhammad Ibraahim (PJMI) is located at Jalan TTS 5/2, Taman Tasik Semenyih, in the Semenyih sub-district of Selangor — a semi-rural area south of Kajang near the Selangor–Negeri Sembilan border. The facility appears to operate as an Islamic-oriented elderly care home serving warga emas in the Semenyih, Beranang, and surrounding kampung catchment. The Taman Tasik Semenyih setting is considerably quieter than Klang Valley urban nursing homes, with a different community character that may suit residents who find institutional urban environments disorienting.

Semenyih has expanded as a residential area with new township development, but hospital access remains oriented toward Kajang — Hospital Kajang is approximately 20 kilometres away — or further into the Klang Valley for specialist care. Families in Semenyih, Semenyih Baharu, Broga, and the surrounding area may find PJMI a practical local option that reduces travel time for regular visits, a meaningful factor given the benefit of frequent family contact for elderly residents in long-term care.

PJMI holds a 4.8-star rating from 75 Google reviews — a notably high rating for a rural-corridor facility at a review volume that carries real evidential weight. Publicly available operational information is limited compared with urban nursing homes, which makes direct enquiry and a personal visit more important than usual. Families should ask about capacity, care scope, monthly fees, JKM licence status, and whether the facility can support their relative's specific medical conditions. Speaking with other families currently using the facility is advisable before committing to placement.

source_data.address: Jalan TTS 5/2, Taman Tasik Semenyih, 43500 Semenyih, Selangor
source_data.notes: Islamic-oriented; semi-rural Semenyih; limited public operational info — visit essential

facilities.name: Golden Mansion Senior Care Centre
facilities.editorial:
Golden Mansion Senior Care Centre is located at No. 7 Jalan SS3/39 in Taman Universiti, Petaling Jaya — a residential neighbourhood in the western PJ corridor roughly equidistant from Subang Jaya Medical Centre and Assunta Hospital. The centre positions itself across three care categories: nursing home, assisted living, and retirement home — a tiered model that allows residents to move between care levels as needs change without necessarily relocating. The residential street setting is typical of converted-house nursing homes in PJ.

The western PJ location is well positioned for families in Taman Universiti, SS3, and the Subang corridor who prefer a nursing home within a familiar neighbourhood rather than an industrial-zone facility. Proximity to both Subang Jaya Medical Centre and Assunta Hospital provides two escalation options within a short drive — relevant for residents with conditions requiring regular specialist review or whose status may change acutely.

Golden Mansion holds a 4.9-star rating from 72 Google reviews — a strong signal at a review volume that provides reasonable evidential confidence for a care home of this size. Families should visit to assess the physical layout, confirm which care level applies to their relative's current condition, ask about pricing across room types and what is included in the monthly fee, and verify current staffing levels and care protocols for conditions requiring active nursing intervention. Confirm current availability early, as well-reviewed homes in this corridor tend to be in demand.

source_data.address: No. 7 Jalan SS3/39, Taman Universiti, Petaling Jaya
source_data.notes: Three care tiers: nursing home, assisted living, retirement home; near SJMC and Assunta Hospital

notes: Batch 6: Top 10 standalone facilities by review count. Phase B begins. Columbia Asia (hospital-licensed, not Akta Pusat Jagaan) treated as eldercare facility. AustinLoyal 1.9/260 handled neutrally per editorial rules. ECON Perling: largest NH group in SG+MY. Amazing Grace Caring Home: different network from Amazing Grace (Subang) batch 1. PJMI: limited public info, rural setting.
